title: Molarity Calculator (mol/L to moles)
shortDescription: Calculate moles of solute from molarity and volume.
description: Use this molarity calculator to find the moles of solute in a solution from its molar concentration and volume. Enter molarity in moles per liter (mol/L) and volume in liters (L) to get the result in moles (mol).
metaDescription: Free molarity calculator: enter molarity (mol/L) and volume (L) to find the moles of solute. Fast, accurate chemistry concentration math.

result.label: Moles of solute
result.empty: Enter molarity and volume to see the moles of solute.

sections.heading: What is molarity?
paragraphs: Molarity is a measure of concentration that describes how many moles of solute are dissolved in one liter of solution. It is expressed in moles per liter (mol/L), often written as M.
To find the amount of solute, multiply the molarity by the volume of the solution: moles = molarity (mol/L) × volume (L).

sections.heading: When to use this calculator
paragraphs: This tool is useful in chemistry labs and classrooms whenever you know a solution's concentration and volume and need the number of moles of solute.
Knowing the moles lets you convert to mass using the molar mass, or plan dilutions and reactions accurately.

howTo.name: Enter the molarity
howTo.text: Type the molar concentration of your solution in moles per liter (mol/L).

howTo.name: Enter the volume
howTo.text: Type the volume of the solution in liters (L).

howTo.name: Read the result
howTo.text: The calculator multiplies the values and shows the moles of solute (mol).

faq.a: Moles of solute = molarity (mol/L) × volume (L). Rearrange it to solve for molarity or volume if needed.
Enter molarity in moles per liter (mol/L) and volume in liters (L). The result comes out in moles (mol).

faq.q: How do I convert milliliters to liters?
faq.a: Divide the volume in milliliters by 1000. For example, 250 mL equals 0.25 L.

faq.q: How do I get the mass of solute?
faq.a: Multiply the moles of solute by the molar mass (g/mol) of the substance to obtain the mass in grams.

faq.q: Is molarity the same as molality?
faq.a: No. Molarity is moles per liter of solution, while molality is moles per kilogram of solvent.
